nodes.label: AI as scaffolding for initial project structure
nodes.grounding_span: I'll typically use it for boiler plate code and for generating class and method structures when I'm first starting out a new project

nodes.label: AI as guide for unfamiliar libraries
nodes.grounding_span: I started by asking the AI which functions within LibLouis would be required for my application

nodes.label: AI for rapid GUI prototyping
nodes.grounding_span: asked the AI to make a super basic GUI using WPF for me so that I can test how the wrappers worked

nodes.label: AI as code improver on human-written logic
nodes.label_negative: AI generating complex logic from scratch
nodes.grounding_span: written the algorithm myself to begin with, then asked the model to improve upon it

nodes.label: AI as global averaging machine
nodes.label_negative: AI as source of exceptional, boundary-pushing code
nodes.grounding_span: I simply see it as a global averaging machine; it can pump out great code that any average person can produce, not great code that exceeds the average

nodes.label: AI for compartmentalized feature implementation
nodes.grounding_span: I could very easily compartmentalize each individual feature of the application and simply ask the model to add GUI features consecutively

nodes.label: Personal codebase familiarity and ownership
nodes.grounding_span: I want to be very familiar with my own codebase as it becomes more complex

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: it saved me a bunch of time and effort

nodes.label: Deep understanding of complex logic
nodes.grounding_span: some types of complex logic are very hard for even the best of programmers to wrap their heads around without trying it out for themselves

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for GUI prototyping
nodes.grounding_span: I think I was most satisfied with AI when trying to build a mobile port

nodes.label: Frustration with over-reliance on AI for complex logic
nodes.grounding_span: I spent more time re-writing the algorithm to actually understand what it did than I saved using the AI model to generate it for me

nodes.label: Skepticism about AI's ability to exceed average
nodes.grounding_span: I don't believe the AI would be able to create an enterprise-grade application on par with that of an actual UX designer

nodes.label: Steady pragmatic use of AI as it improves
nodes.grounding_span: As AI gets better, I'll just use it the same way I currently do

nodes.label: Compartmentalization and sequential decomposition
nodes.grounding_span: I could very easily compartmentalize each individual feature of the application and simply ask the model to add GUI features consecutively

nodes.label: Preference for understanding through personal implementation
nodes.grounding_span: some types of complex logic are very hard for even the best of programmers to wrap their heads around without trying it out for themselves
